subject.title: @ai-sdk/vue / Chat: tool-input-delta for OpenAI customTool({ format: { type: 'text' } }) does not live-update tool input (stays undefined   until final tool call)
subject.body:
### Description

I’m seeing a streaming UI mismatch with OpenAI custom freeform tools (text format).

When using @ai-sdk/openai openai.tools.customTool({ format: { type: 'text' } }) and rendering with @ai-sdk/vue Chat, the browser network
stream clearly contains tool-input-delta chunks, but the tool input in the UI does not update live during input-streaming.

It only appears when the final tool-call / tool-input-available arrives.

Expected behavior
During input-streaming, tool input should update progressively for freeform text tools (for example raw HTML, SQL, shell text), not only
for JSON-like inputs.

Actual behavior
part.input remains undefined while deltas stream, then jumps to final value at the end.

Minimal repro
Backend:
```ts
const tools = {
setHtml: openai.tools.customTool({
  name: 'setHtml',
  description: 'freeform html input',
  format: { type: 'text' },
}),
};
```
Frontend:
```ts
const chat = new Chat({
transport: new DefaultChatTransport({ api: '/api/chat' }),
});
```
Render tool part input during input-streaming:

```
<ToolInput v-if="part.input !== undefined" :input="part.input" />
```


**Observed stream**
tool-input-delta chunks are present in network/devtools (for example "SELECT * ", "FROM users " or raw HTML fragments).

**Why this seems to happen**
process-ui-message-stream currently parses accumulated tool-input-delta via partial JSON parsing and assigns parsed value to input. For
non-JSON freeform text deltas, parse result is often undefined until the end.

**Suggested fix**
For non-JSON/freeform tool inputs, preserve streaming raw text in UI state (for example fallback to raw text when partial JSON parse
fails), so input-streaming is actually visible for text tools.

### AI SDK Version

ai: 6.0.105
@ai-sdk/vue: 3.0.105
@ai-sdk/openai: 3.0.37
Vue 3 app, OpenAI Responses API custom tools (format: { type: 'text' })

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct
